Authorship and Title Accuracy.Definition: Determine whether the poster clearly and completely displays all core information of the paper (complete paper title, all authors' names) without any errors (including typos in the title, omissions/errors in authors' names, incorrect order of authors, errors in unit labeling, etc.)?
Authorship and Title Accuracy.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, clearly displays the complete paper title and all authors' names without any typos, omissions, or ordering errors; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, the title is complete, the authors' names are basically complete, with only extremely minor errors (such as punctuation errors) that do not affect information recognition; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, the title is incomplete, there are omissions or obvious errors in authors' names, which affect the recognition of core information; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, no paper title or no authors' names, or there are serious errors (such as completely inconsistent title, all authors' names are wrong).

Image Uniqueness and Quality.Definition: Are the images free of duplication, and their visual clarity sufficient to meet the needs of display and information transmission?
Image Uniqueness and Quality.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, all images are free of duplication, with high clarity, and details and data labels can be clearly identified without blurriness or distortion; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, images are free of duplication, with good clarity, core details can be identified, and slight blurriness does not affect information reading; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, there are some duplicate images, or the image clarity is poor, and core details cannot be identified; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, images are severely blurred and distorted and cannot be identified, or all are duplicate images.

Balanced White Space.Definition: Determine whether the overall distribution of white space on the poster is balanced and reasonable, avoiding local overcrowding of content (text and images piled up without gaps) or excessive blank spaces (large areas without content) to ensure visual comfort.
Balanced White Space.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, white space is evenly distributed, the ratio of content to blank space is coordinated, visually comfortable, without overcrowding or excessive blank spaces; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, white space is basically balanced, with only extremely minor local overcrowding or blank spaces that do not affect the overall visual experience; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, white space distribution is unbalanced, with obvious local overcrowding or excessive blank spaces that affect visual comfort; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, the whole is severely crowded without white space, or there are large areas of excessive blank spaces, making normal reading impossible.

Contextual Relevance.Definition: Determine whether the images in the poster are closely related to the research theme through reasonable layout and arrangement, and form a logical echo with adjacent text content (such as experimental methods, result analysis), helping readers understand the research content and improve overall readability.
Contextual Relevance.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, all images are highly relevant to the research theme, reasonably laid out, and closely logically echo with adjacent text, which can effectively assist in understanding the content; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, images are relevant to the theme, layout is basically reasonable, and there is a certain logical echo with adjacent text, which does not affect overall understanding; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, some images have weak relevance to the theme, layout is chaotic, and there is no logical echo with adjacent text, affecting understanding; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, images are irrelevant to the research theme, have no logical connection with the text at all, and cannot assist in understanding.

Optimal Visual-to-Text Ratio.Definition: Determine whether the proportion of images and text in the poster is reasonable. On the premise that the images are consistent with the theme and text context, prioritize ensuring sufficient image proportion to more intuitively convey research content (such as experimental results, research models).
Optimal Visual-to-Text Ratio.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, the proportion of images and text is balanced, the image proportion is sufficient, and all images are consistent with the theme and text context, which can efficiently convey research content; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, the proportion of images and text is basically reasonable, the image proportion is moderate, consistent with the theme and context, and can normally convey research content; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, the proportion of images and text is unbalanced, the image proportion is insufficient, or some images are inconsistent with the context, making it impossible to effectively convey research content; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, no images at all, or the image proportion is extremely unreasonable, making it impossible to convey research content through the combination of images and text.

Dimension Appropriateness.Definition: Determine whether the overall size of the poster is suitable for conventional display environments (such as academic conference display boards, campus exhibition areas) and matches the content volume, avoiding extreme aspect ratios (too long, too narrow, too short) that affect the display effect and reading experience.
Dimension Appropriateness.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, the size is suitable for the display environment, matches the content volume, the aspect ratio is coordinated, and the display effect is excellent; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, the size is basically suitable for the display environment, basically matches the content volume, and the aspect ratio has no obvious abnormalities; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, the size has low adaptability to the display environment, or does not match the content volume, and the aspect ratio has obvious abnormalities; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, the size is extremely unreasonable (such as too narrow and long, too short and small), making normal display and reading impossible.

Visual Consistency.Definition: Determine whether the poster's color scheme, typesetting style (font, line spacing, alignment), and design elements (borders, icons, dividing lines) maintain a unified style while following academic norms (conciseness, solemnity), forming a clear visual identity.
Visual Consistency.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, the color scheme is concise and solemn, the typesetting is standardized and unified, the design elements have a consistent style, the visual identity is strong, and it conforms to academic norms; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, the color scheme, typesetting, and design elements are basically unified, and slight inconsistencies do not affect the overall visual effect, conforming to academic norms; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, the color scheme is messy, the typesetting is not unified, the design elements have a chaotic style, the visual identity is weak, but basically conforms to academic norms; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, the color scheme is exaggerated, the typesetting is chaotic, the design elements are contrary to academic norms, and there is no visual consistency.

Content Fidelity.Definition: Determine whether the data, formulas, professional terms, research findings, experimental conclusions and other contents presented in the poster are completely consistent with the original paper, without tampering, omission, or incorrect expression.
Content Fidelity.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, data, formulas, terms, research findings, etc. are completely consistent with the original paper, without any tampering, omission, or error; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, the core content is consistent with the original paper, with only extremely minor expression differences (without affecting the original meaning), no tampering or omission; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, there are partial data tampering, term errors, or omissions of research findings, which affect the authenticity of the content; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, the core data, formulas, and conclusions are seriously inconsistent with the original paper, with extensive tampering or errors.

Information Flow Logic.Definition: Determine whether the poster content is organized in an intuitive and reasonable academic logical order, and whether it can guide readers to clearly sort out the narrative context of the entire research without logical gaps or messy content.
Information Flow Logic.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, the logic is coherent, the order is reasonable, the context is clear, readers can quickly sort out the whole picture of the research without any logical gaps; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, the logic is basically coherent, the order is basically reasonable, with only minor logical connection problems that do not affect overall understanding; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, the logic is chaotic, the order is messy, there are obvious logical gaps, and it is difficult to sort out the research context; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, there is no logical order at all, the content is messy, and it is impossible to sort out the research context.

Self-Contained Explanation.Definition: Determine whether the poster content is complete and easy to understand, and readers can independently understand all core content of the poster only by reading the text and images of the poster without the oral explanation of the presenter.
Self-Contained Explanation.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, the content is complete and expressed concisely and clearly, without the need for oral explanation, readers can independently understand all core content; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, the core content is complete, the expression is relatively easy to understand, and only individual details need oral supplementation, which does not affect overall understanding; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, the core content is missing, the expression is obscure, and a lot of oral explanation is needed to understand the core content; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, the content is incomplete, the expression is chaotic and obscure, and it is impossible to independently understand any core information.

Rationality of Cognitive Load.Definition: Determine whether the information presentation of the poster is consistent with the human working memory capacity, without redundant information accumulation (such as irrelevant text, repeated content) or irrelevant visual interference (such as redundant colors, messy decorations), to avoid cognitive overload and reading fatigue of readers.
Rationality of Cognitive Load.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, the information is concise and refined, without any redundant content and irrelevant visual interference, and there is no cognitive pressure in reading; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, the information is relatively concise, with only a small amount of redundant content or slight visual interference, which does not affect the reading experience; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, there is a lot of redundant content or obvious visual interference, which is likely to cause cognitive fatigue; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, the information is seriously accumulated, the irrelevant interference is extremely strong, making normal reading impossible, and the cognitive load is extremely high.

Identifiability of Information Hierarchy.Definition: Determine whether the core information of the poster (research innovation points, core conclusions, key data, core methods) has clear visual highlighting designs (such as bolding, enlarging, color distinction, position highlighting, boxed labeling, etc.), and the primary and secondary levels of information are clear, allowing readers to quickly capture the core content.
Identifiability of Information Hierarchy.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, the core information is prominent, the visual enhancement design is clear, the primary and secondary levels are clear, and readers can quickly capture the core content; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, the core information has a certain visual highlighting design, the levels are relatively clear, and the core content can be captured with a simple browse; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, the core information has no visual highlighting design, the levels are vague, and it is difficult to capture the core content; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, there is no distinction of information levels, the core information is mixed with non-core information, and it is impossible to capture the core content.

Extractability of Core Knowledge.Definition: Determine whether the poster sets clear cognitive extraction clues (such as subheadings, bullet points, visual labels, etc.) for the key research information (research questions, core methods, core experimental results, innovative value), allowing readers to quickly locate and extract core knowledge.
Extractability of Core Knowledge.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, the extraction clues of key information are clear, each core knowledge point has a clear identifier, and readers can quickly locate and extract all core knowledge; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, the key information has extraction clues, the identifiers of core knowledge points are relatively clear, and core knowledge can be extracted quickly; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, the key information has no clear extraction clues, the identifiers of core knowledge points are vague, and it is difficult to locate and extract; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, there are no extraction clues at all, and it is impossible to locate and extract any core knowledge.

Adaptability of Visual Cognition.Definition: Determine whether the layout of images and text and the visual flow of the poster conform to the conventional human reading and visual cognitive habits (such as left-to-right, top-to-bottom visual flow, matching of image and text space, avoiding frequent visual jumps), reducing cognitive loss caused by visual jumps.
Adaptability of Visual Cognition.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, the layout of images and text is reasonable, the visual flow follows conventional reading habits, there are no frequent visual jumps, and the cognitive loss is extremely low; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, the layout of images and text is basically reasonable, the visual flow basically follows conventional habits, and slight visual jumps do not affect cognition; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, the layout of images and text is chaotic, the visual flow deviates from conventional habits, there are frequent visual jumps, and the cognitive loss is relatively large; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, the layout of images and text is disorderly, the visual flow is chaotic, making normal reading impossible, and the cognitive loss is extremely large.

Friendliness of Term Cognition.Definition: Determine whether the professional terms in the poster are adaptively processed according to the academic communication scenario, and whether necessary concise explanations are provided for non-general terms and uncommon terms in the field, which are neither redundant nor cumbersome, and can avoid cognitive obstacles caused by unfamiliar terms.
Friendliness of Term Cognition.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, terms are used standardizedly, concise and accurate explanations are provided for non-general and uncommon terms, there are no cognitive obstacles, and it is not redundant; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, terms are used standardizedly, most non-general terms have explanations, and a small number of unexplained terms do not affect overall cognition; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, some non-general and uncommon terms have no explanations, or the explanations are cumbersome and redundant, causing certain cognitive obstacles; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, a large number of non-general and uncommon terms have no explanations, terms are used non-standardizedly, and it is impossible to understand the core content.

Traceability of Research Logic.Definition: Determine whether the causal relationship and derivation logic of the research in the poster (such as the connection between experimental design and experimental results, the derivation between research methods and core conclusions, the supporting relationship between data and viewpoints) have clear visual/text identifiers, allowing readers to trace back in a forward direction without cognitive logical gaps.
Traceability of Research Logic.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, the research logic identifiers are clear, the causal relationship and derivation process are clear, readers can trace back completely in a forward direction without any logical gaps; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, the research logic identifiers are relatively clear, the causal relationship and derivation process are basically clear, and slight logical gaps do not affect traceability; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, the research logic identifiers are vague, the causal relationship and derivation process are unclear, there are obvious logical gaps, and it is difficult to trace back; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, there are no logical identifiers at all, the causal relationship and derivation process are chaotic, and it is impossible to trace back.

Memory Retention of Key Information.Definition: Determine whether the core research information of the poster (innovation points, key data, core conclusions) is presented through concise expression, visual enhancement (such as bolding, simplification, chart summary), etc., which helps readers' short-term memory retention and long-term knowledge extraction.
Memory Retention of Key Information.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, the core information is concisely expressed, the visual enhancement is in place, easy to understand and remember, which helps short-term retention and long-term extraction; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, the core information is relatively concisely expressed, has a certain visual enhancement, basically easy to remember, and short-term retention can be achieved; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, the core information is cumbersome to express, has no effective visual enhancement, is not easy to remember, and it is difficult to achieve short-term retention; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, the core information is chaotically expressed, has no visual enhancement, cannot be remembered, and cannot be extracted.

Adaptability Across Cognitive Levels.Definition: Determine whether the poster can take into account readers with different cognitive levels in the field (such as senior researchers, young scholars, postgraduates, entry-level learners), and on the premise of ensuring research depth, allow entry-level readers to understand the core research content without excessive cognitive thresholds.
Adaptability Across Cognitive Levels.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, taking into account readers with different cognitive levels, sufficient research depth, entry-level readers can understand the core content without cognitive thresholds; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, basically taking into account readers with different cognitive levels, sufficient research depth, entry-level readers can understand the core content through simple sorting out; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, only suitable for senior researchers, entry-level readers find it difficult to understand the core content, with high cognitive thresholds; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, the cognitive threshold is extremely high, only a few senior researchers can understand, and most readers cannot understand the core content.

Immediacy of Cognitive Feedback.Definition: Determine whether the poster allows readers to immediately judge whether they understand the core information during the reading process through summary sentences, core viewpoint labels, key conclusion extraction, etc., avoiding the vague state of 'reading but having no cognitive gain'.
Immediacy of Cognitive Feedback.Scoring Reference: 5 points: Fully compliant, there are clear summary sentences and core viewpoint labels, readers can immediately judge their own understanding, and have clear cognitive gains after reading; 3-4 points: Basically compliant, there is certain summary content, readers can roughly judge their own understanding, and have basic cognitive gains after reading; 1-2 points: Non-compliant, lack of summary content, readers find it difficult to judge their own understanding, and the cognitive gain after reading is vague; 0 points: Completely non-compliant, there is no summary content at all, readers cannot judge their own understanding, and have no cognitive gain after reading.
